story: Few months after his arrest, Greg sits in solitary on his nineteenth birthday, replaying the raid that shamed his grandmother, Lita, and worrying who will help her with groceries. A recent phone call revealed a new weakness in her voice. Across town, Lita had collapsed on the stairs the night before; neighbors called an ambulance, and St. Mary’s kept her overnight for observation. The slot in Greg’s door opens. Givens, the unit supervisor, hands him a red-velvet cupcake and says he’s too smart for prison. He lays out steps if Greg wants to change: get him out of solitary and off B Block, try for kitchen duty, sign up for G.E.D. classes, then the new College Annex Program with real credits.
implausible_knowledge: Across town, Lita had collapsed on the stairs the night before; neighbors called an ambulance, and St. Mary’s kept her overnight for observation.
question: What will Greg most likely ask the staff to do first?
answer_with_knowledge: Beg for an immediate phone call to St. Mary’s Hospital to check on Lita and speak with her doctors.
answer_without_knowledge: Ask to start the transfer off B Block and get signed up for G.E.D. and the College Annex program.
